validation_commands.details: Changed files stop at FTacticalGame, tactical tests, DesignNotes, and artifacts; no planned UI files are part of the delivered feature.

blocking: Preview-click resolution is not implemented: FTacticalGame still uses the old adjacent-hex stopped-ship facing path, and runtime code does not consume getStoppedShipPreviewHeadingsForHex(...) to map preview-route clicks back to implied starting headings.

UI rendering/forwarding is not implemented: FBattleBoard still draws only movement/left/right route buckets and FBattleScreen still forwards only those legacy accessors, so selecting a stopped mover does not immediately show preview routes from every legal starting facing.
Move prompt text still instructs the player to choose an adjacent hex first, which conflicts with the approved preview-first interaction model.
warning: The verifier PASS is scoped to the additive model/data change and cites a narrower plan context than the governing feature plan, so it is not sufficient evidence of full feature completion.
Regression coverage validates the new model surface but does not cover the planned preview-click runtime behavior or the planned tactical UI rendering/prompt updates.
note: DesignNotes now documents the additive preview-route model surface accurately, but earlier adjacent-hex UI notes remain, matching the delivered code rather than the intended feature plan.

follow_up_feature_requests: Implement the remaining stopped-ship-move-ui plan work by forwarding FTacticalGame preview-route metadata through FBattleScreen to FBattleBoard, rendering all legal stopped-ship preview routes immediately on selection, and updating move-phase prompt text to the preview-first interaction.

Extend stopped-ship movement selection so clicking any highlighted preview-route hex resolves the implied starting facing and continues the normal movement-selection flow, then add regression coverage for preview-click resolution and updated UI behavior.
summary: The delivered work completes the additive stopped-ship preview-route model surface and preserves existing non-stopped and MR==0 behavior, but it does not deliver the governing plan's preview-click and tactical UI changes, so the feature is not complete.
